[S1] Welcome back to Market Pulse. Today we've got a lot to unpack.
[S2:excited] The S&P just closed up 2.3% on a massive NVDA earnings beat!
[S1:curious] So what's actually driving that kind of move? Is it AI demand, or something else?
[S2] Earnings came in at $0.89 versus the $0.74 estimate, and guidance was revised higher for Q3.
[S1:serious] But here's the thing -- $1.2T in market cap added in a single session is not a healthy kind of rally.
[S2:concerned] Right. And we're seeing unusual flows into SPY and QQQ, with IBIT getting outsized volume.
[S1] Meanwhile, oil dropped 3.5% and the DXY is testing multi-month lows.
[S2:warm] For folks just tuning in, DXY is basically a measure of how strong the dollar is against a basket of other currencies.
[S1:playful] A strong dollar makes your European vacation cheaper -- a weak dollar makes Swiss chocolate feel like a luxury good.
[S2] On the crypto side, BTC is pushing back above $72K and ETH is holding $3,800.
[S1:curious] Is this the start of a new leg up, or just chop?
[S2:serious] Look -- with sanctions pressure rising and geopolitical risk elevated, I wouldn't read too much into a single session.
[S1] That's a fair read. We'll see if the FOMC minutes on Wednesday change the picture.
[S2:warm] Thanks for listening, folks. See you tomorrow.
